summarization.a: The scatter plot shows the relationship between growth temperature and refractive index, with data points from this work  for lower temperatures (below 200 °C) and other studies for higher temperatures (~150°C - 500°C). This study shows stronger positive correlation between temperature and refractive index.

a.question: Which trend (this study or other studies) shows stronger correlation between growth temperature and refractive index?
What is the maximum refractive index achieved in this work?
a.answer: The maximum refractive index achieved in this work is approximately 1.60, occuring at 175°C.

a.question: Which growth temperature among those studied in this study appears to produce optically denser films? What supports this conclusion?
a.answer: Among the temperatures studied in this work, the highest growth temperature (~175 °C) appears to produce the optically densest films. This is supported by the maximum refractive index measured in this study. A higher refractive index indicates improved greater optical density.

a.question: Does increasing the growth temperature above 300°C seem to improve optical density?
